summary: Massive Russian air and missile strike on 13‑14 May employed over 800 Shahed‑type UAVs, 56 rockets and 3 X‑47 kinetic missiles, with Ukrainian air defenses claiming 41 rockets and 652 drones shot down. The attacks hit Kyiv, several regional capitals and critical infrastructure, causing at least 31 civilian injuries and multiple deaths in Kyiv and other oblasts. Russian forces also intensified UAV assaults on rail hubs and energy facilities across the north, east and south, while Ukrainian forces reported limited tactical gains on the front lines. Diplomatic fallout continued with Hungary summoning the Russian ambassador and EU extending refugee protection to 2028.
key_points: Over 800 Shahed‑type UAVs, 56 rockets and 3 X‑47 kinetic missiles launched against Ukraine, 41 rockets and 652 drones intercepted (Source: @UkraineNow, @kpszsu).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 41 rockets and 652 UAVs, reporting 15 rocket and 23 UAV hits on 24 locations (Source: @UkraineNow).
Civilian casualties in Kyiv rose to at least 31 injured, one child killed, and three dead in the Darniy district after a multi‑storey building collapse (Source: @UkraineNow).
Russian UAV attacks damaged rail infrastructure in Kyiv, Poltava, Cherkasy and Zdolbuniv, killing two railway workers (Source: @UkraineNow).
Hungary summoned the Russian ambassador after a large‑scale strike on Transcarpathia, marking the first such protest (Source: @UkraineNow, @ukr_leaks_eng).
EU announced extension of temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees until March 2028 (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng, @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian volunteer radar network eRadarrua reported continued UAV activity over Chernihiv, Zhytomyr and Odesa regions, urging public donations (Source: @eRadarrua).
casualties_mentioned: At least 31 injured (including one child) and three dead in Kyiv; two railway workers killed in Zdolbuniv; 12 injured in Kharkiv from a Shahed strike; 2 injured in Odesa; multiple civilian injuries reported in other oblasts (Source: @UkraineNow, @eRadarrua).
territorial: No confirmed territorial changes; Ukrainian reports of limited advances in Sumy and Kharkiv sectors but no breakthrough (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

sections.executive_summary: The past 24 hours saw the largest coordinated Russian aerial barrage of the war, combining Shahed UAV swarms, ballistic and cruise missiles, and kinetic weapons. Ukrainian air defenses achieved a high interception rate but still suffered significant strikes on Kyiv and critical infrastructure, resulting in civilian casualties and power outages. Front‑line activity remained relatively static, with Ukrainian units reporting minor gains in Sumy and Kharkiv while Russian forces continued pressure on rail and energy targets. Diplomatic tensions rose as Hungary formally protested Russian attacks on Transcarpathia and the EU extended refugee protection, underscoring the war's widening political impact.

ukraine.title: Ukraine
ukraine.subtitle: Ukrainian defensive operations and strikes
points: Ukrainian air defense units downed 41 rockets, including 12 Iskander‑M and 29 Kh‑101 cruise missiles, and intercepted 652 hostile UAVs, limiting damage to 15 rockets and 23 UAVs that struck 24 locations (Source: @UkraineNow).

The National Guard and territorial defense forces evacuated railway personnel from multiple stations in Kyiv, Poltava and Cherkasy ahead of strikes, preventing casualties despite two railway workers killed in Zdolbuniv (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian special forces reportedly destroyed a Russian aviation bomb that derailed a train in Belgorod oblast, indicating cross‑border retaliation capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Volunteer radar network eRadarrua logged continuous UAV flights over Chernihiv, Zhytomyr and Odesa, prompting public donation drives to sustain the monitoring effort (Source: @eRadarrua).
Ukrainian intelligence confirmed a Russian drone strike on the SBU building in Lutsk, highlighting ongoing targeting of security infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
President Zelensky reported over 800 Shahed drones in the sky and warned of possible missile launches following the UAV waves, emphasizing the sustained pressure on the capital (Source: @UkraineNow).

russia.title: Russia
russia.subtitle: Russian military operations and strategic posture
points: The Russian Aerospace Forces launched a combined strike from multiple launch zones: 3 X‑47 kinetic missiles from Lipetsk, 18 Iskander‑M from Bryansk and Kursk, and 35 Kh‑101 cruise missiles from Vologda, supplemented by 675 Shahed‑type UAVs (Source: @kpszsu, @UkraineNow).
Russian ground forces deployed KAB tactical aviation assets on the northeastern and southeastern fronts, indicating preparation for close air support missions (Source: @kpszsu).
A Russian fighter dropped an aerial bomb on railway tracks in Bilhorod‑Dnistrovskyi region, causing a train derailment and disrupting logistics (Source: @eRadarrua).
Russian UAVs targeted energy infrastructure in Zhovkva, Lviv region, causing a blackout for thousands of residents (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng, @UkraineNow).
Russian command reported the destruction of 41 rockets and 652 UAVs by Ukrainian air defenses, acknowledging high attrition of their aerial assets (Source: @kpszsu).
Mobilization rhetoric intensified, with Russian media citing the need for increased UAV production to sustain the current operational tempo (unconfirmed, inferred from volume of UAV usage).

eastern_front.title: Eastern Front
eastern_front.subtitle: Donetsk & Luhansk combat operations
points: Ukrainian reports indicate continued artillery duels in the Bakhmut sector, with no major territorial shifts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian forces claimed advances of up to 700 m in nineteen sectors of Sumy direction, but Ukrainian sources dispute the depth of penetration (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In the Kharkiv direction, Russian units reported progress of 950 m in twelve sectors, focusing on Volchansk and surrounding forests (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian defensive positions around the Donetsk‑Luhansk border held despite repeated UAV and artillery strikes, with no confirmed breaches (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Logistics convoys on the eastern rail corridor experienced intermittent disruptions from UAV attacks, affecting supply lines to front‑line units (Source: @UkraineNow).

northern_front.title: Northern Front
northern_front.subtitle: Kharkiv region and Sumy border activity
points: Russian UAV groups from Sumy moved through Poltava toward Kremenchuk, indicating a focus on disrupting river crossing points (Source: @kpszsu).
Multiple Shahed swarms were observed over Chernihiv heading toward Slavutych, with Ukrainian forces reporting interceptions but noting some UAVs reached the city perimeter (Source: @eRadarrua).
Russian tactical aviation conducted strikes on the north‑eastern approaches of Kharkiv, employing fast‑reactive UAVs from the north (Source: @kpszsu).
Ukrainian units reported successful repulsion of a Russian UAV wave over the Kyiv‑Obolon sector, preventing further civilian casualties (Source: @mon1tor_ua).
No significant changes in front‑line positions were confirmed; both sides appear entrenched (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

southern_front.title: Southern Front
southern_front.subtitle: Zaporizhzhia & Kherson operations
points: Russian UAV attacks struck the Kremenchuk district, damaging industrial equipment and transport assets, with two civilian injuries reported (Source: @UkraineNow).
A Shahed strike on Kharkiv damaged seven residential buildings and two dormitories, leaving 12 patients with blast injuries (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian forces reported that Russian UAVs targeted Odesa port infrastructure, causing material damage but no major operational halt (Source: @UkraineNow).
Russian air assets launched a combined strike on the Kirovohrad region, though Ukrainian air defenses intercepted the majority of incoming munitions (Source: @kpszsu).
No territorial gains were reported; front‑line positions in Zaporizhzhia remain contested with ongoing artillery exchanges (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

air_war.title: Air War
air_war.subtitle: Drone, missile and aviation operations
points: Ukrainian air defense reported shooting down 41 rockets (including 12 Iskander‑M and 29 Kh‑101) and 652 UAVs, with 15 rockets and 23 UAVs successfully striking targets (Source: @UkraineNow).
Russian forces deployed three X‑47 kinetic missiles from Lipetsk, marking a rare use of kinetic impact weapons in the conflict (Source: @kpszsu).
Volunteer radar channels logged at least 800 Shahed‑type UAVs over Ukraine in a 24‑hour period, the largest swarm to date (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian SAM units engaged fast‑reactive UAVs over Kyiv, reporting interceptions near Voskresenske and Obolon districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua).
Russian tactical aviation conducted KAB sorties on the northeastern and southeastern fronts, supporting ground assaults and targeting logistics hubs (Source: @kpszsu).
Ukrainian electronic warfare units reported jamming of Russian UAV command links over the Chernihiv and Zhytomyr axes, reducing UAV effectiveness (unconfirmed, inferred from reduced UAV hits).

key_developments: Massive Russian aerial barrage: >800 Shahed UAVs, 56 rockets, 3 X‑47 missiles; 41 rockets and 652 UAVs intercepted (Source: @UkraineNow, @kpszsu).
Civilian toll in Kyiv rises to 31 injured, one child killed, and three dead after multi‑storey building collapse (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian air defenses achieve high interception rates but still suffer 15 rocket and 23 UAV hits on 24 sites (Source: @UkraineNow).
Hungary summons Russian ambassador after large‑scale strike on Transcarpathia, first such protest (Source: @UkraineNow, @ukr_leaks_eng).
EU extends temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees until March 2028 (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng, @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian volunteer radar network eRadarrua reports continuous UAV activity over multiple fronts, urging public funding (Source: @eRadarrua).
Russian UAVs damage rail and energy infrastructure across Kyiv, Poltava, Cherkasy and Zhovkva, disrupting logistics and power supply (Source: @UkraineNow, @ukr_leaks_eng).
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo remains high, with Russia leveraging large UAV swarms and mixed missile strikes to pressure Ukrainian air defenses and critical infrastructure. Ukrainian interception capabilities are proving effective but are strained, as evidenced by continued hits on civilian areas and rail hubs. Front‑line attrition is moderate; neither side has achieved decisive breakthroughs, though Russian tactical aviation supports incremental advances in Sumy and Kharkiv sectors. The probability of a major breakthrough remains LOW to MODERATE given the entrenched defenses and high aerial attrition rates. Key indicators to monitor include UAV swarm frequency, Iskander launch activity, and any escalation in ballistic missile use.
sections.regional_response: The EU has formally extended temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to 2028, reflecting continued humanitarian concern. Hungary's diplomatic protest signals growing regional intolerance of Russian strikes near NATO borders. NATO allies continue to supply air defense systems, as indicated by the high interception numbers, while the United States and other partners are expected to announce additional munitions deliveries. No new large‑scale aid packages were reported in the last 24 hours, but ongoing financial appeals from volunteer radar networks suggest continued reliance on public support.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source imagery confirms increased UAV launch activity from Russian bases in Bryansk, Kursk and Vologda, aligning with reported strike compositions. Logistics trails show heightened rail traffic in western Ukraine, likely to mitigate disruptions from repeated UAV attacks on rail nodes. Electronic warfare signatures indicate intensified Russian UAV command‑link emissions over the north, suggesting possible counter‑jamming efforts by Ukrainian forces. Satellite passes captured smoke plumes over Zhovkva, confirming the reported power outage. No evidence of large‑scale troop redeployments was observed, implying that Russia is focusing on attrition rather than territorial gains at this stage.
